op_000001.source_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (n : ℕ), bc.number (bc.ket n) = (n : ℂ) • bc.ket n
op_000001.formalized_statement: ∀ {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℂ V] (bc : BosonCore V) (n : ℕ), bc.number (bc.ket n) = (n : ℂ) • bc.ket n
op_000001.statement_formalization_notes: Direct formalization of the target using the existing `BosonCore.number` and `BosonCore.ket` definitions.

op_000001.proof_sketch: Case on n. For n = 0, unfold the number operator and use vacuum annihilation. For n + 1, rewrite the composed operator application with Module.End.mul_apply, then apply the existing ladder lemmas a_ket_succ and aDagger_ket together with linearity.
op_000001.proof_text:
cases n with
| zero =>
    simp [BosonCore.number, Module.End.mul_apply, bc.ket_zero, bc.vacuum_annihilate]
| succ n =>
    rw [BosonCore.number, Module.End.mul_apply, bc.a_ket_succ, map_smul, bc.aDagger_ket]
op_000001.lean_error_fingerprint: AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.lean:10:8: error(lean.unknownIdentifier): Unknown identifier `n` | AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.lean:10:2: error: Tactic `cases` failed: major premise type is not an inductive type | ?m.21

op_000001.proof_sketch: Introduce the parameters first. For `n = 0`, the goal reduces to `number_vacuum` after rewriting `ket 0 = vacuum`. For `n + 1`, unfold `number` and use `a_ket_succ`, linearity of `aDagger` via `map_smul`, and then `aDagger_ket`.
op_000001.proof_text:
intro V _ _ bc n
cases n with
| zero =>
    simpa [BosonCore.ket_zero] using bc.number_vacuum
| succ n =>
    rw [BosonCore.number, Module.End.mul_apply, bc.a_ket_succ, map_smul, bc.aDagger_ket]
